client_role: Use returned official identity and reference paths
security_note: Identity, schema interpretation, and access-scope derivation must rely on keyId rather than client hints.

client_role: Send integrity token derived from keyId
server_role: Verify integrity token before accepting the request
security_note: Production should use a signed or JWT-style check to detect corruption or forgery.

client_role: Use provisional local identifier until the official keyId is returned
server_role: Preserve value without treating it as authoritative identity
security_note: Production servers may apply basic validation such as length and character checks.

client_role: Use as a temporary local hint before the server responds
server_role: Derive authoritative kind from keyId with unique identifiers removed
security_note: Do not trust client-supplied kind when it conflicts with keyId.

client_role: Optionally provide an ISO language code per value node
server_role: Preserve and validate optional language metadata
security_note: Different nodes in the same document may use different languages.

client_role: Send latest known version when writing
server_role: Generate and compare versions for optimistic sync
security_note: Reject writes based on older versions.
Useful for comments, chat, and other chronological views.

client_role: Use for ordering, support, and stale-state diagnosis
security_note: Useful for understanding what changed after version mismatches.

client_role: Send and consume protocol-level string payload
server_role: Store and return protocol-level string payload
security_note: Interpret richer formats above the protocol layer without changing the transport type.
